entry.title: SAC Excel Addin - Formula Error
entry.content:
Hi SAC Community, 

We have designed SAC Budget Input templates on SAC Excel Addin (with similar approach of what SAP BPC EPM Excel used to do).

With this Budget data input template, user selects required entity and budget version based on SAC formula =SAP.GETDIMENSIONFILTER("Table2","Company_Code")

This formula works fine for all users but the same formula gets converted to (becomes dummy function for excel as it can not read the formula ) following =_xldudf_SAP_GETDIMENSIONFILTER("Table2","Company_Code") for few users (all users have same SAC access profile / role with Excel addin READ, WRITE, DELETE)

Please reply if similar/same issue faced by any of you and have a resolution for it. 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account and getting started in the community
entry.content:
Hello David,

I am interested in joining The digital skills initiative program for women.I took a sabbatical for almost 10years and I am keen in scaling up my SAP Skills, I would need your help on how I can enroll myself in the program, kindly guide.

 

Thank you'

Sarada.

entry.title: Subcontracting PO for a segmented material where the vendor is another plant within the same system
entry.content:
Experts,

I seek your guidance in mapping the following business flow within the system. Additionally, if you have any links that provide a step-by-step procedure, it would be greatly appreciated.

The flow involves creating a subcontracting purchase order for a segmented material where the vendor is another plant within the same system.

Thank you

Ravi K

entry.title: CSV file to SFTP server using SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Expert ,

I have a requirement where I am supposed to send a csv testing_Files from my local to an SFTP server in a CSV file using CPI iflow.

Any idea how may I proceed on this further?

thank you in Advance

Best Regards

entry.title: SDK Version 1400 does not support Crystal Enterprise version 1100 (FWM 02032)
entry.content:
Hi Team,

When we tried to upgrade Crystal Report XI R2 Server to Crystal Report Server 2020 using the Promotion Management Tool (PMT), we encountered problems. Specifically, we encountered an error stating that Crystal Enterprise version 1100 (FWM 02032) could not be supported by SDK Version 1400 if the old server (Crystal Report XI R2 server) was chosen as the source in the PMT.Next, we attempted to install Java SDK 8 on the CR 2020 server. Upon checking, we discovered that the identical process had encountered the same issue.

 

Please guide us .

Thanks

Ramesh

entry.title: Re: Stock Transfer between two EWM Managed plants assigned to same warehouse
entry.content: Hi Usman, we are having same issue. could you please let me know if you have any solution

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework
entry.content: Hi @Rene_de_Daniel and @everyone, this version of the SAP EAF has advanced the state-of-the-art, spurring interesting questions and critiques in the responses to this blog thus far. I look forward to contributing to this endeavour. Good work!

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting Automatic Payment Program - F110
entry.content:
Hello, 

I'm facing issue similar as mentionned in scenario 5. We've created our own PDF form for cheque printing.

I've done 2 tests: 

1. Payment method C, PMW format: Check, form: FI_PAYM_DE_CHECK
When I launch the sapfpaym program, I'm getting this message: No payment Media Created in Format Check. 
2: Payment method C, PMW format: Check, form: ZFIFAE850015_CHECK_UK(our specific PDF form)
When I launch the sapfpaym program, I'm getting this message: FORM ZFIFAE_00498CHECK cannot be used for output management. 
Do you have any idea on what can help me resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: Vectorize your data for Infuse AI in to Business using Hana Vector and Generative AI
entry.content: Hello Julian!! Amazing Blog!!!! I can handle the chunks ok, but when I execute the HANA_DB i get this error.

entry.title: Re: Control cycle for EWM IM managed slocs
entry.content: Hi All, could you please let me know the process

entry.title: Embracing Sustainability with SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Introduction

Sustainability has been defined as “meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs” by UN World Commission on Environment and Development. In practical terms, it can mean the ability to manage natural resources well over the long term. Better processes & systems can help in managing today's needs without depleting resources or causing harm to the environment, economy, or society. Sustainability involves 3 main aspects of Environment, Socials & Economics.

  • Environmental Sustainability aims at conserving natural resources, reducing pollution, and protecting ecosystems.
  • Social Sustainability focuses on human rights, fair labor practices & seeks to ensure that everyone has equal access to resources and opportunities necessary for a healthy and fulfilling life.
  • Economic Sustainability is about creating systems and practices to balance economic development & resource use while maintaining a healthy environment.

The essence of Sustainability is to create a harmonious balance between these 3 pillars to ensure that current and future generations can thrive without compromising the planet's health or societal well-being.

From an organizational perspective, there is increasing consumer pressure on companies to target Carbon Zero Future. There are more & more regulations (like European Sustainability Reporting Standards or ESRS, Human Rights Due Diligence, Supply Chain Act Germany) to meet targets in line with Paris Agreement & COP26. Investor expectations are rising for significant Scope 3 (indirect emissions) reduction efforts. Up to 90% of a company’s environmental footprint lies in its supply chain – meaning that the supply chain represents a major opportunity for companies to further their environmental, social and governance (ESG) objectives. Refer Value chain and supply chain sustainability for details.

The purpose of this post is to highlight the topic of Sustainability for discussions. It provides brief introduction to the subject, touches upon the Sustainability solutions from SAP and provides high level inputs & resources around Sustainable Procurement with SAP Ariba.

 

SAP & Sustainability

SAP's purpose is to help the world run better and improve people's lives with sustainability at the core. In line with it, SAP offers a wide array of Sustainability solutions for its customers to embrace sustainability effectively. Solutions span across topics like ESG reporting, Climate action, Circular economy, Social responsibility, CSRD & Sustainable procurement. With these solutions, there is an opportunity to positively influence 12 of the 17 UN defined sustainability goals. Some of the prominent sustainability solutions from SAP are as below.

Green Ledger

It is a solution designed to help businesses accurately account for the carbon they produce across their value chain. It integrates environmental data into financial processes, thus enabling organizations to manage their sustainability performance as effectively as they manage the financials. For quick product overview, refer this 3 minutes video.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM)

This solution helps businesses measure, manage, and reduce their environmental impact along the value chain. It enables organizations to calculate and track their carbon footprint, water usage, and other environmental metrics. It is a cloud application running on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). For quick product overview, refer this 2 minutes video.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT)

It is cloud solution to help businesses monitor and manage their sustainability performance. It helps organizations to track, analyze, and optimize their sustainability initiatives & achieve goals like reducing carbon footprint, delivering on green line performance, and meeting diversity and inclusion targets. For quick product overview, refer this 2 minutes video.

SAP Sustainability Data Exchange (SDX)

It is a solution designed to enable secure and efficient exchange of sustainability-related data between businesses and their partners. It aims to enhance collaboration, compliance & transparency across the entire supply chain by enabling organizations to share and access verified sustainability data seamlessly. It is a based on SAP Business Network & built using the technical framework of GreenToken by SAP.

It standardizes data exchange by following technical specifications of the Partnership for Carbon Transparency (PACT) framework of the World Business Council of Sustainable Development (WBSCD). It also offers interoperability with other equivalent networks using WBCSD PACT standards. Companies can use SAP SDX along with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Alternatively, if businesses already have a solution that calculates carbon footprints effectively, they can use SAP SDX to share this data securely. For more details on the solution, kindly refer these links.

  • Quick Product Overview (2 minutes video)
  • SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
  • SAP Sustainability Data Exchange

 

SAP Ariba & Sustainability

Adopting digitalization helps in sustainability by providing the tools and insights needed to operate more efficiently, reduce waste, and make informed decisions that benefit the environment and society.  For overall guidance on embracing Sustainability in procurement processes, refer this 10 minutes video.

Using SAP Ariba in itself reduces paper work and help with resource optimization contributing to sustainability. Also, with its wide range of solutions & strong collaboration capabilities, SAP Ariba can help an organization further to achieve its Sustainability goals while working with the supplier base. Additionally, new solutions & features help to take it even further. Below figure attempts to present various ideas through which SAP Ariba can contribute to Sustainability goals.

Embedding Sustainability in Source to Pay Process

 

Other Related Topics

Sustainable Supplier Finance

Sustainable Supplier Finance program helps & finances buyers to incentivize supplier who adopt sustainable practices. Buyers can apply a pricing benefit for suppliers who meet predefined sustainability criteria. Taulia, a SAP company, offers solution to make this effective for companies. Buyer can easily track the suppliers’ ESG performance and incentivize them to improve it. Kindly refer this link for more details.

Solution Extension - Sustainability for SAP Ariba

This solution offers SAP Ariba customers the ability to enhance functionality and integrate applications that support procurement sustainability goals. It is a Partner solution from Cordis Solutions, a partner company of excelerateds2p & is available on SAP Store. Solution is a comprehensive collection of packaged services, pre-built content, enhanced configuration, and complimentary extensions built on BTP to support a new or existing Ariba customer on their sustainability journey. For more details, refer Sustainability for SAP Ariba

Ecovadis

EcoVadis is the universally recognized assessment platform which rates businesses' sustainability based on 4 key categories: environmental impact, labor & human rights standards, ethics, and procurement practices. It provides 2 solution extensions as below for SAP Ariba which are available on SAP Store.

  • EcoVadis Business Sustainability Assessments, ESG Ratings and Carbon Scorecards

This SAP Store App for Suppliers can help them to add their sustainability rating in SAP Business Network profile. It can help Suppliers stay compliant & also boost their sales. You can also refer to this Blog Post from Amanda @amandalaldin for more details - Standing out as a sustainable supplier with SAP Business Network and EcoVadis

SAP Business Network Supplier Profile with Ecovadis Ratings

  •  EcoVadis Business Sustainability Ratings for SAP Ariba

This SAP Store App integrated with SAP Ariba Supplier Risk helps you review the ESG risk and performance of your Suppliers with robust and reliable sustainability scorecards, and take action to reduce the carbon footprint. Risk scorecard can directly be seen from the Ariba SLP Supplier 360 dashboard. This direct integration helps to make informed decisions & take positive actions based on sustainability targets resulting in higher efficiency and more sustainable value chains.

Risk Scores in SAP Ariba SLP Supplier 360

AI & Sustainability

Sustainability & Profitability go hand in hand, and AI can play a crucial role in embedding them together. SAP aims at using SAP Business AI to help customers optimize for sustainable impact across their full value chain, enabling the intelligent, sustainable enterprise, powered by the pool of foundational business data. AI can help deciding on data-driven ESG and net-zero goals, strategies, and actions. It can also help complying with regulations and automate sustainability reporting. Few existing use cases already exists and others can be build using SAP BTP. Kindly refer below resources for more details.

  • The power of sustainability, ERP, and AI - This 6 minute video explains how integrating sustainability into ERP business processes, combined with the power of SAP Business AI, can help an organization’s business decision-making to the next level and meet environmental, social, and governance compliance requirements.
  • Building AI and Sustainability Solutions on SAP BTP - This SAP Learning (learning.sap.com) Course helps building skills to infuse AI in Sustainability solutions. Its around 10 Hours in duration and is free for all.
  • Putting Sustainability at the Heart of ERP - SAP recently announced 2 new AI-driven sustainability use cases - Emission Factor Mapping with AI & ESG Report Generation with AI. This article has more details.

 

Conclusion

In today's world, sustainability is no longer an option but a necessity for organizations aiming to thrive and remain competitive. SAP Ariba provides a platform to help businesses achieve business goals while integrating sustainability into their procurement and supply chain operations. It helps businesses to make informed decisions that positively impact the environment while driving economic value.

By adopting SAP Ariba, digital transformation and sustainability can go hand-in-hand. Organizations can contribute to a more sustainable world and create long-term value for all stakeholders. In summary, embracing sustainability with SAP Ariba is not just about compliance and efficiency, it's about redefining business success in the context of our global responsibility. By embedding sustainable practices into core operations, businesses can pave the way for a more sustainable and prosperous future for all. It can help the world run better and improve people's lives with sustainability at the core.

 

Consolidated List of Resources

  • SAP's Sustainability solutions
  • UN defined sustainability goals
  • Green Ledger Overview video
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM) Overview video
  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) Overview video
  • SAP Sustainability Data Exchange (SDX) Overview video
  • Sustainability in Procurement Processes video
  • Sustainable Supplier Finance
  • Solution Extension - Sustainability for SAP Ariba
  • EcoVadis Business Sustainability Assessments, ESG Ratings and Carbon Scorecards
  • EcoVadis Business Sustainability Ratings for SAP Ariba
  • The power of sustainability, ERP, and AI video
  • Training Course - Building AI and Sustainability Solutions on SAP BTP
  • Article - Putting Sustainability at the Heart of ERP

 

Note: Kindly refer 'Comments' for updates to the Blog Post. Also requesting readers to enter Comments with info which can be helpful to all.

entry.title: Re: The value of the 'Tax' output parameter of the formul...
entry.content:
Hi, @Felipe_Lima,

 

When using another tax code that uses the same IPI BC ICMS formula, it worked correctly.

I looked for a very similar tax code, which uses the same formulas and it worked.

The only difference is PIS and COFINS.

In green tax code, with formula IPI BC ICMS error, and in red tax code with formula worked.

Have you had any experience with this type of situation?

Regards,

 

Carlos Santos

entry.title: Re: Validate if the swift/bic field is empty through a BADI in tx fi02
entry.content:
I got it!

Thanks for your response

entry.title: Re: Common standard features not working in RAP OData V4 - S4 2022 FPS02
entry.content: I found similar differences in my RAP Application as well. the one that bothered me the most is the Child Entity Object Page is not loading in V4 version, but it loads fine in V2 Version. Any expert comments/recommendations will be appreciated

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

My name is Cephus Legall-Brickey. I am a 32 year old black male in the IT Support field currently. I have been recently coming to terms with my status as a neurodivergent individual after spending the majority of my life ignoring and underestimating its real impact.

My passion lies in the amazing versatility of technology and its applications, with an emphasis in Cybersecurity GRC. To this end I have self taught myself Sec+, Network+ and A+, as well as kept continual tabs on new an emerging computer hardware and software in the personal and enthusiast spheres. However, I have not had the resources or opportunity to realize this goal and move myself beyond consumer IT support. Now, one resource I have in temporary abundance is time, and I aim to use it to realize this career path.

Yesterday, I had a meeting with a career coach who recommended I look into SAP. I was surprised to see that its software had security and GRC applications in the corporate scene. Delving in further, I came across this initiative.

Along with a number of other resources that I have been pointed towards, I believe this initiative will allow me to officially begin a career in GRC consultation by leveraging the values of SAP software in tandem with separate cybersecurity qualifications. I would like to formally express my desire to take part and be an impactful member in the forward momentum of this community.


Thank You,

Cephus

entry.title: Re: Best way to isolate BOPF unit testing from the DB
entry.content:
Hi everyone. I am also trying to create unit tests for our BO using /BOBF/CL_TOOL_TEST_DOUBLE_SM however when I create a child class inherited from /BOBF/CL_TOOL_TEST_DOUBLE_SM the class is compiled but unit test doesn't run. I have a following warning message:

 

Syntax errors in program [/BOBF/CL_TOOL_TEST_DOUBLE_SM==CP] prevent analysis of test classes in program [ZTEST_BOBF_TD]

 

at the same time class /BOBF/CL_TOOL_TEST_DOUBLE_SM gives no syntax errors.

I also noticed that in the table TAUNIT_PROG_INFO this class has BAD_RESSOURCE /BOBF/CL_TOOL_TEST_DOUBLE_SM==CP value. I have no clue what it means too.

Did someone faced thing like this.

Here is a simple code which doesn't work in our system:

 

 

REPORT ZTEST_BOBF_TD.

class ltc_main definition for testing risk level harmless duration short.
  private section.
  methods td for testing.
endclass.

class ltc_td definition inheriting from /BOBF/CL_TOOL_TEST_DOUBLE_SM for testing.

endclass.

class ltc_main implementation.
  method td.
    new ltc_td( ).
  endmethod.
endclass.

 

 

I have also noticed that if I copy /BOBF/CL_TOOL_TEST_DOUBLE_SM to a new class like ZCL_TOOL_TEST_DOUBLE_SM one to one without any modifications, then using this new class in own scripts works very well.

Does someone have ideas why? 

Thanks!

entry.title: MDG Consolidation with active records
entry.content:
Hi MDG Experts,
We have created custom template for consolidation with Active records and strategy set as 'Remove duplicates'
After activation step, Best record is updated as per Best record calculation logic and other potential duplicate records are marked for deletion but, Is there any table to get those details like, which records are marked for deletion, which records were selected as selection step, which records were outcome of matching step ?

I am able to check activate record through Manage consolidation tile, but didn't more information through that tile.
As Process is successfully activated now, I am not see data of matching step.

Please guide here with tables/Function module to get those information.

Thanks,
Brijeshkumar

entry.title: Re: Connecting Multiple Employee Central Instances to a Single SAP S/4HANA System for Employee Data
entry.content:
Hello Paramita,

Thank you for the update.

Now reviewing this, looks great for minimaster scenaries where organizational objects are seldom used and there are no timeoff use cases. I'm trying to understand the restrictions how can be managed:

"You cannot use point-to-point replication of time management data from Employee Central in the current version if you enable this feature. You can also replicate organizational objects from only one Employee Central instance currently."

Any suggestions?

Do this means that positions, organizations, mut be managed centrally?

Thanks again!!

Rodolfo

entry.title: Re: Upload General Journal Entries - incomplete header entry
entry.content: Added S/4HANA Cloud for Finance tag, Please remove tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Re: LSMW : Unable to Specify Files . Logical path incorrect ?
entry.content:
Hi,

the field 'physical path' have to be just <FILENAME> not the full path as you did. This is a placeholder to be overwritten by SAP during runtime.

entry.title: Re: SAML Configuration for BOBJ BI Launchpad (4.3 SP03).
entry.content:
Hi @liuzipeng ,

are you still facing this issue, if yes kindly check file "securitycontext.xml" located at location  <bobj install directory> /tomcat/webapps/BOE/WEB-INF.

 

Regards,

Utkarsh

entry.title: SAP: A 50-year history of success
entry.content:
On April 1, 1972, five former IBM employees - Dietmar Hopp, Hasso Plattner, Claus Wellenreuther, Klaus Tschira, and Hans-Werner Hector - started the company System Analyse Programmentwicklung (System Analysis Program Development). Their idea was to create standard enterprise software that integrated all business processes and enabled data processing in real time.

 SAP's founders and employees worked closely with customers - often sitting side-by-side with employees in customers' offices to learn their business needs and processes. By 1975. they had built applications for financial accounting (RF). invoice verification. and inventory management (RM). Some of their early customers were the nylon factory belonging to ICI in Ostringen, Germany, Knoll. Burda. Linde. and Schott. The blend of real-time data processing, standardization. and integration were the basis for SAP's transformation from a small German company into a global leader in business software. In 1979, the company started developing R/2, the second generation of its software. In 1980, SAP's roughly 80 employees moved into their first own office building in Waldorf, Germany.

From R/3 to Global Player

Even while R/2 was enjoying huge sales success and one year before SAP went public with an IPO in 1988 – the company’s managers were looking ahead to its third generation of software. The SAP R/3 success story began in 1992, with the client-server software smoothing the path to a globalized economy, turning SAP into a global player with subsidiaries and development centers across the world.

 In 1999, SAP responded to the Internet and new economy by launching its mysap.com strategy. Ten years later, the company branched out into three markets of the future: mobile technology, database technology, and cloud. To rapidly become a key player in these new domains, SAP acquired some of its competitors, including Business Objects, Sybase, Ariba, SuccessFactors, Fieldglass, and Concur.

Into the Cloud with SAP HANA

In 2011, the first customers started using the in-memory database SAP HANA. Data analyses that used to take days or even weeks were now completed in seconds. Four years later, SAP launched SAP S/4HANA, its latest generation of business software, running entirely on SAP HANA. SAP is committed to enabling every enterprise to become intelligent, networked, and sustainable – bringing together the solutions, technology, and best practices needed to run integrated, digital business processes in the cloud.

 SAP offers choice across the four largest hyperscale cloud vendors. The company’s integrated applications connect all parts of a business into an intelligent suite on a digital platform. SAP Business Technology Platform brings together application development, data and analytics, integration, and AI into one platform. It is a central element of SAP’s “RISE with SAP” offering.

 SAP has approximately 269 million cloud users currently, more than 100 solutions covering all business functions, and the largest cloud portfolio of any provider. SAP operates 57 data centers at 32 locations in 15 countries.

entry.title: Re: activate IDOC service path in S4 for IDOC receiver connection with CI
entry.content: Sounds like a reason for escalation because suggesting any form of custom handling for basic IDoc integration is ludicrous, and there is no reason to fall back to file-based IDocs when there is a perfectly viable HTTP handler available in the system.

entry.title: Re: Connect PO7.5 to Integration Suite?
entry.content: Can it be done... sure. Should it... probably not. If you are referring to certain integrations as "complicated" that suggests the existence of technical debt that you'd potentially be increasing by some multiplier.  I'd take this as an opportunity to simplify.

entry.title: Re: Common standard features not working in RAP OData V4...
entry.content: Try deploying the app. I've found, deploying a V4 RAP app resolves almost all of these.

entry.title: Re: Question for SVT Tracking Programs Using 'Initial' op...
entry.content:
Hello

first: check the OSS Note docu (check SAP marketplace)

second: check e.g. https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-members/substance-volume-tracking-using-sap-ehs-management/ba-p/13272665

Third. You need to seperate the "fill" from the "tracking" part

To use SVT the "master data" must be present. Without that: the solution us "useless" (e.g. you need to know: which material is relevant; you need to have composition data on REAL_SUB level)

Regarding tracking: you can use SVT in two basic modes: only tracking; and tracking with blocking

If the data is not "good" enough: Tracking is the best option (no blocking)

There is a "retraking" report which you can use to do "retracking" (if data is better). => Check SVT docu to finde this ABAP routine. But if the data is "really"  bad: than initial tracking is better

Wish you success in SVT

C.B.

PS: CBRC20 etc. can be used to control: what object need to be "registered". If you start with SVT you can use this option as well. BUT the tracking success depends HEAVILY on CORRECT Master Data.

entry.title: Re: How to handle References in EHS Rule Editor
entry.content:
Hello

what exactly do you try to do? Normally the Expert Rule Story is NOT used for handling "Referencing" and "Inheritance"

The issue is: what is the "rule".... Expert Rule sets are normally used to write direct data to the object (e.g. REAL_SUB).

Nether heard about the use of "Rule sets" to handle "Referencing" (using "Custom like Rule Set code).

The only exemption: if you buy ! Rules... then the "Dangerous Goods" regulations can be supported DG_CL_SUB assignments to REAL_SUB).

Can you explain what you would like to get?

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Erro na F110 para fornecedor sem imposto
entry.content: Muito obrigada pela rapidez e ajuda!

entry.title: Re: BW/4HANA Cockpit slowness
entry.content:
Hi @nachtaktiv and @roland_szajko  

Our Basis team follow the instructions of SAP Note 2918492 - FAQ: BW/4HANA 2.0 Cockpit Setup assigning the following roles to the user:  SAP_BW4_MODELER and SAP_BW4_REPORTING_USER.

Hope it helps.

entry.title: SAP BTP Integration Suite trial account and interfaces developed vanish after 64 days
entry.content:
Has this happened to anyone else, i created the  Integration Suite trial on 30th May, have done a lot of interfaces, had close to 29 published interfaces with over a 250+ messages with API`s and all were working fine. Today morning i log in and i noticed the Integration suite service was not in my subscriptions and everything was nuked. I had to setup the trial again and this time it tells me i have 26 days left.

What happened to all my interfaces. I ran the booster again and now it says capabilities are not activated, ok so i ran the booster again, which completed successfully, i redid all the roles again- same result. 

This is what i find interesting, on the account i see a message that the current trial of 90 days expires in 26 days, indicating integration suite account was opened on 30th. However the paragraph also says your trial account will be deleted in 56 days from now, this is painful. I know in all likelihood, nothing will be done as its a trial account, just lost a lot of learning.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Integration Suite trial account and interfaces developed vanish after 64 days
entry.content: Kindly move this to the right community, i am not able to do it.

entry.title: How to upload a file in the attachment folder of a freight order in TMS for S4?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I have a requirement to upload a file in the attachment folder of a fright order in TMS for S4 using inbound proxy.

I am trying to use this proxy TransportationEventBulkNotification_In but I am getting an error

Also I checked this forum (How to update a link in the attachment folder of a... - SAP Community) and tryed to replace the link information to File information but I am not getting success on this.

Can you please help me?

entry.title: Re: import monitor error ehp8
entry.content: Hello, I solved it, the problem was that many tables are replicated, so I deleted all the tables indicated by the registry and tried the installation again.

entry.title: Re: import monitor error ehp8
entry.content: Hello, I solved it, the problem was that many tables are replicated, so I deleted all the tables indicated by the registry and tried the installation again.

entry.title: how can I streaming in xml to json converter on field level
entry.content:
I am using xml to json converter with following config....

Input xml:

<root>
<records>
<profitCenter>100100</profitCenter>
<validTo>9999-12-31</validTo>
<controllingArea>ABC1</controllingArea>
<validFrom>2023-01-01</validFrom>
<createdOn>2023-11-10</createdOn>
<createdBy>I2193</createdBy>
<longText>Chemicals</longText>
<profitCenterText>Chemicals</profitCenterText>
<profitCenterName>CHEMICALS</profitCenterName>
<profitCenterCompanyCodes>1000</profitCenterCompanyCodes>
<profitCenterCompanyCodes>1825</profitCenterCompanyCodes>
</records>
<records>
<profitCenter>567327</profitCenter>
<validTo>2024-01-02</validTo>
<controllingArea>ABC1</controllingArea>
<validFrom>2023-11-20</validFrom>
<createdOn>2024-06-26</createdOn>
<createdBy>SAP_WFRT</createdBy>
<profitCenterText>testing</profitCenterText>
<profitCenterName>TESTING</profitCenterName>
<profitCenterCompanyCodes>1000</profitCenterCompanyCodes>
</records>
</root>

Expected output:

{
"records": [
{
"profitCenter": "100100",
"validTo": "9999-12-31",
"controllingArea": "CSX1",
"validFrom": "2023-01-01",
"createdOn": "2023-11-10",
"createdBy": "I2193",
"longText": "Chemicals",
"profitCenterText": "Chemicals",
"profitCenterName": "CHEMICALS",
"profitCenterCompanyCodes": [
"1000",
"1825"
]
},
{
"profitCenter": "567327",
"validTo": "2024-01-02",
"controllingArea": "CSX1",
"validFrom": "2023-11-20",
"createdOn": "2024-06-26",
"createdBy": "SAP_WFRT",
"profitCenterText": "testing",
"profitCenterName": "TESTING",
"profitCenterCompanyCodes": [
"1000"
]
}
]
}

But getting output as:

{
"records": {
"profitCenter":"100100",
"validTo":"9999-12-31",
"controllingArea":"ABC1",
"validFrom":"2023-01-01",
"createdOn":"2023-11-10",
"createdBy":"I2193",
"longText":"Chemicals",
"profitCenterText":"Chemicals,
"profitCenterName":"CHEMICALS,
"profitCenterCompanyCodes":[
"1000","1825"
]
},
"records":{
"profitCenter":"567327",
"validTo":"2024-01-02",
"controllingArea":"ABC1",
"validFrom":"2023-11-20",
"createdOn":"2024-06-26",
"createdBy":"SAP_WFRT",
"profitCenterText":"testing",
"profitCenterName":"TESTING",
"profitCenterCompanyCodes":[
"1000"
]
}
}

The red record should not come.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, July 4th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/07/04/sap-developer-news-july-4th-2024/

SAP Samples on GitHub and the BTP Resource Consumption Monitor repo

  • SAP Samples organization on GitHub: https://github.com/SAP-samples
  • BTP FinOps: Keeping Track of your Credits: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/btp-finops-keeping-track-of-your-credits/ba-p/13737793
  • BTP Resource Consumption Monitor repository: https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-resource-consumption-monitor

Current Customer Engagement Initiatives

  • SAPUI5 2.x: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/adapt-your-app-to-sapui5-2-x-make-an-impact-by-joining-our-cei-project/ba-p/13722775
  • SAP BTP: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3724

July Developer Challenge “Reverse APIs”

  • Main July challenge blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/2024-07-quot-reverse-apis-quot-sap-developer-challenge/ba-p/13749653
  • Task 0 Server and service provisioning https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/2024-07-developer-challenge-task-0-server-and-service-provisioning/m-p/13749996
  • Previous (June) challenge final week discussion https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/submissions-for-quot-sap-hana-cloud-multi-model-quot-developer-challenge/m-p/13744012

SAP Build Governance How-To Guides Published

  • SAP Build Governance Resource Center: https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=bGf3QlX0PEKC9twtmXka9wZKKG5THS9PkxADEwX46a9UQUk5N0dKVjNGWU5FTlFPVDRTRDBLTEMwWC4u
  • Try the SAP Build Governance Resource Center https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/don-t-want-to-start-from-scratch-with-it-governance-try-the-sap-build/ba-p/13729865

The Open Documentation Initiative & the revamped SAP Help Portal comments feature

  • The Grand Prix of Developer Feedback  (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/open-documentation-initiative-blog-posts/the-grand-prix-of-developer-feedback/ba-p/13734863

==============================================================

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP Samples on GitHub and the BTP Resource Consumption Monitor repo

1:11 Current Customer Engagement Initiatives

2:14 July Developer Challenge “Reverse APIs”

3:43 SAP Build Governance How-To Guides Published

5:11 The Open Documentation Initiative & the revamped SAP Help Portal comments feature

TRANSCRIPT

[Antonio] Hola SAP developers. I don't know about you, but I've learned a lot from the sample code that's available in the SAP Samples organization on GitHub. Whatever you're interested in, be it CAP, RAP, AI, connectivity with event brokers, it is likely that you will find a good sample in the reports available there. For example, the latest cool report that I've popped into is the BTP Resource Consumption Monitor by my colleague Willem, who recently also publish a blog post about what the tool can do for you. In essence, keep track of your CPEA, BTPEA credits. The tool has a bunch of things, billing verification, cost breakdown, credit expenditure, monetary analytics, even suggestions for account optimization and cost reduction strategies. And all the code and details to get it running are there for you to explore. Make sure to check it out. I'm including the links in the description below.

[Nico] Hi everyone and welcome to the SAP Developer News. I'm on an early morning walk with the little one, who for some reason seems to be sleeping better outdoors, and I thought I would use the time wisely and record the developer news. So, we would like to point your attention to two customer engagement initiatives that are currently running. The first one is about SAPUI5 2.0. So, the team is looking for feedback and is willing to provide some more insights and help on how to migrate from SAPUI5 version 1 to 2. The second initiative is about SAP BTP and is specifically for SAP BTP admins and solution architects. Again, the team is looking for feedback and is willing to provide some more insights and help with the overall goal of improving the user experience on the platform. These initiatives are really your chance to shape the future of those two products and I very much invite you to participate. and I see you soon, bye.

[DJ] The June Developer Challenge run by my good friend and colleague Vitaly is coming to a close. It's not too late to finish off the tasks though, but July is here and that means we have a new Developer Challenge for you. We're calling it Reverse APIs because essentially It's a reflection on last August's developer challenge on APIs. But this time, it's you that have to create the APIs. API endpoints in services that will be tested by an orthophysic testing mech. Sounds scary? Not at all, because you'll have the power and magic of CAP at your disposal. The whole idea is to learn, to explore, to discover all the features that CAP has to offer when building services and API endpoints. There are ten tasks in this challenge, reflecting ten different API endpoints that you're going to create over three different services over the course of this month, plus a little warm-up task, Task Zero, that is an easy way to start off, and that Task Zero has already been published. head over to the blog post which explains everything and links you to this first task to get going. See you, all right.

[Rekha] Hello, everybody. I hope you're all having a good time. Are you an IT administrator or IT professional looking to streamline and scale your SAP build fusion development efforts? Then it is crucial to have a robust governance framework in place. Effective governance ensures that your organization's software projects are delivered quickly, securely, efficiently, and in alignment with your organizational goals and compliance requirements. SAP has now launched SAP Build Governance Resource Center website. Inside this web page, you will find a package of valuable assets like digital center of excellence toolkit, use case body tool, how to guide for use cases ideation, and fusion development project lifecycle management, guided experience step-by-step tutorial, FAQs, presentation on SAP Build Governance, essential quick links, and SAP Help documentation links for best practices. By consolidating all the resources I just mentioned into a single and easy to navigate platform, SAP Build Governance Resource Center empowers you to operate and scale your Fusion development programs with confidence. I hope you found this update exciting. Thank you.

[DJ] A couple of years ago, we launched the Open Documentation Initiative to allow, to encourage you to collaborate with us on documentation, either by providing feedback or contributing fixes and improvements to the documentation via the universal global content collaboration mechanism and platform that is GitHub. Recently, you might have noticed that there's also been some revamping of an existing feature directly on the SAP Help Portal. That's the comments feature which some may find more straightforward to use to contribute comments while perusing the documentation. We want to know what you prefer. Jens Hailey has written a blog post all about this new revamp and also describing what we want to achieve by asking you to help us find out the difference between the two approaches. Read all about it in Jens's blog post. The link is in the description.

entry.title: Re: how can I streaming in xml to json converter on field...
entry.content:
You have designated only "specified ones" for your configuration so you would need to add an extra designation for /root/records to get the desired output.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: 2024-07 Developer Challenge - Task 0 - Server and service provisioning
entry.content:
My submission for task 0.

https://alpesa-simplest-deployment.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/

entry.title: Fiori app not displaying - Vocabulary Annotation File 'C_MRPCTRLRBYAOR_SRV_VAN' version '0001' not f
entry.content:
Intro

Some times in the Fiori app you may get blank screen. 

For FIORI apps we need to maintain the service in background. So that these app will work. At first we have to get error by finding the service name which is responsible for the respective app.

Follow the below steps to solve the issue.

You can find the error 'Vocabulary Annotation File 'C_MRPCTRLRBYAOR_SRV_VAN' version '0001' not found on SAP GW Server' in tcode /n/IWFND/ERROR_LOG - SAP Gateway Error Log 

Find the service name from the bottom screen of tcode /n/IWBEP/REG_VOCAN by using the Vocabulary annotaion

Then go to tcode /n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and click add service.

Give the system alias (LOCAL as Default) and Technical service number (Taken from above step) and click enter. Then select the service and click add service.

Then choose the respective package and save it.

Then Run the below program

  • /n/IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP
  • /n/IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP

Then reopen the app to get the screen. Now the system will show the app.

Also have a look on below sap notes

2826598 - How to resolve Vocabulary Annotation File 'xxxxxx' version '0001' not found?

SAP Note 1986626 before activating your service for the soft state

Thanks

entry.title: How to add a Restriction Type in a Business Role?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We need to know how to add a Restriction Type on Business Role. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to add a Restriction Type in a Business Role?
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

In order to add a Restriction Type in your Business Role, you need to add a Business Catalog that contains this Restriction Type.

You can check this following the steps below:

  1. Go to Display Restriction Types app
  2. Search for the Restriction Type
  3. Open the Restriction Type
  4. Check "Business Catalogs" tab
  5. You need to add one of these Business Catalogs to your Business Role

Kind regards, 

SAP Support.

entry.title: How to Configure Odata Integration in AppGyver with your SAP b1 Base API URL
entry.content:
Hi,
I am new on AppGyver and trying to set Odata integration but it's giving the error "The entered URL is not recognized as a valid OData service metadata" Can anyone please explain the steps (With screenshots maybe) on how to configure Odata integration in AppGyver with you SAP b1 credentials?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps for SAP Business One, the next-gen Development Environment
entry.content:
It giving error when I enter this URL: "https://opensap.sapbusinessonecloud.com:50000/b1s/v2"
Error: "The entered URL is not recognized as a valid OData service metadata"

Can you please provide the latest Base API URL that works now?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Why does my field rename not work in a view
entry.content: Found it, one has to rtename the fields too in the form started with the pencil sign.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Launchpad tile counter problem after "Save as tile"
entry.content: Hi, did you found solution to this issue ?

entry.title: Why can't I select Hierarchy in the Preview
entry.content:
Hello,

Althought when looking in the Analytical model I can see that my dimension has a Hierarchy associated.

 

 

But in preview I can't select the hierarchy (For a very short while it worked) and must have changed something, bit doesn't apear anymore...

What could be wrong, everything is deployed ...

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Hyperlinking
entry.content:
Hi there

 

Great article. I have been using hyperlinks from one page to another applying the cascading filters from a chart to a detailed report and it works well. Do you know of a way to remove the linked analysis Story Filter via code after it is added? It doesn't seem to remove like a dimensions filter or a variable and doesn't seem to be accessible via Analytical Applications code.

 

Any advice would be great!

entry.title: NO RFC_AUTHORIZATION for functional module RPY_EXISTENCE_CHECK_FUNC in SAP BW Bridge
entry.content:
Hello,

  We are getting error which trying to perform a shell conversation of ADSO from on-premise BW 7.5 to SAP BW Bridge. The error message is " NO RFC Authorization for function module RPY_EXISTENCE_CHECK_FUNC". 

 

Appreciate your help.

Many thanks,

Balaji

entry.title: Re: Customer for function SP may not have account group Message no. VP303
entry.content: Hi Lakshipathi ji, I have been following you for years now and very impressed with your SAP knowledge. You are doing great service to people like me. But I like to request you to tone down sometimes your replies. I understand people ask sometimes very trivial questions and don't do research before they ask question or sometime, they just don't have the knowledge. But you are GURU and we expect you to be kind and show compassions towards us. Thanks

entry.title: How to define the flexible workflow for parked supplier invoices (not save as completed) ?
entry.content:
Hi, 

We need to create workflow for parked invoice, I tried workflow with save as complete, but it was not useful. Is there anyway to create workflow for parked invoice? 

best regards, 

Laci

entry.title: Re: CSV lookup using CPI
entry.content: Solved using Poll Enrich step

entry.title: Re: CSV lookup using CPI
entry.content: Solved using Poll Enrich step

entry.title: Use Instarem coupon code: gv3vUW for quick $75 discount on first order
entry.content:
Use Instarem referral code: { gv3vUW } to get a $73 discount on your first transfer with Instarem. Use below steps to redeem this offer:
  • Sign up for an Instarem account using Instarem referral code: [ gv3vUW ]
  • During the registration process, enter the Temu referral code: gv3vUW
  • Complete your first transfer using the Instarem platform.
  • This Instarem promo code: { gv3vUW } work for all 2024 and 2025

The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following: Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests: Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods. Increase the Maximum Number of Connections: You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance. Use a More Efficient HTTP Client: Consider using a more efficient HTTP client library that can handle a larger number of connections without causing the error.

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Custom Algorithmic Key Figures Solution for FORECAST CONSUMPTION, with a “BACKLOG” 🏃
entry.content:
Thanks for share it! Is very helpful and detailed.

Regards

Zule

entry.title: Dupicated regions for Mexico
entry.content:
We are implementing SAP in a company, and in the DEV system the regions (states) for the country of Mexico are duplicated.

How can this be fixed, We have not start using it yet.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Reduce time to market with Hosted Pages in Customer Data Cloud (CDC)
entry.content:
Great blog

In the CLP option would the DY need to implement session checking like accounts.session.verify JS and some webSDK confin in CDC login page to recirect back to DY site?

entry.title: BAPI_DISPUTE_CREATE not linking the dispute case with the open item
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using BAPI_DISPUTE_CREATE to create dispute cases via interface. Our dispute case is getting created with all the parameters we have passed and the linked objects are also getting updated but when we go to the FBL5N report we are not able to see the dispute case linked to the open item. 

Is there any additional BAPI we need to run or any parameter we need to pass to link the dispute case with the customer invoice?

Parameters we are passing in the BAPI - 

CASE_GUID_CREATE

CASE_TYPE

UPDATE_TASK = X

AMOUNTS_DELTA - DELTA_ORIGINAL, DELTA_DISPUTED and DISPUTE_CURR

ATTRIBUTES - PROCESSOR, FIN_BUKRS, CASE_TITLE, FIN_KUNNR and DISPUTE_CURR

OBJECTS - VBRK, BSEG (BUKRS+BELNR+GJAHR) and KNA1

entry.title: Re: Upload General Journal Entries - incomplete header entry
entry.content: It is an informational message and should not prevent the journal entry postings. However, you can review your template in the header section to ensure dates, references, and company codes are populated. You can also try posting a manual journal entry using the same data to check if everything is ok.

entry.title: Re: How to do data modelling of 200+ plus tables in IDT i...
entry.content:
this cannot be a reasonable business-request with 200 tables in IDT !!!
do the raw-data-connection/processing in the source-system/DB e.g. with SQL and finally use these result(s) to build a clean semantic-model in IDT.

entry.title: Use $200 Temu coupon bundle [ACT513469] or {frm126057} to get free temu items
entry.content: Use $200 at Temu coupon bundle codes {act513469} or {frm126057} in 2024 to get free temu items. If you're new, make sure to sign up using referral code [act513469] and apply the coupon when checking out for your first purchase. The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following: Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests: Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods. Increase the Maximum Number of Connections: You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance. Use a More Efficient HTTP Client: Consider using a more efficient HTTP client library that can handle a larger number of connections without causing the error. Optimize Your Application: Review your application's performance and optimize it to reduce the load on the system. This may involve improving database queries, reducing the number of API calls, or using caching mechanisms. Contact SAP Support: If none of the above solutions work, you can contact SAP support for further assistance. They may be able to provide more specific guidance or help you troubleshoot the issue. Remember to carefully evaluate the impact of any changes you make to your system's configuration to ensure that they do not compromise performance or security.

entry.title: Re: Factura en Crystal Reports

entry.title: Re: "Position Vacant Since" Report
entry.content: Hi Anish053 I hope you are doing well, I have the same need, I need to get the date or time that a position became vacant, have you been able to do it?

entry.title: Re: How to call a GUI Transaction in SAP Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
Thanks @alexey_arseniev for the blog.

For this case, where this is the starting parameter in the transaction I want:

how would you configure the Target Name in FLPD? I tried SINGLE_SEARCH_VEHICLE_GV, but the value is not populated

entry.title: Re: Data Federation in Datasphere
entry.content: I just learned that Data Federation in SAP Datasphere is a game-changer! It uses virtualization to unify data sources in real time without consolidating them, enhancing agility and preserving data integrity. It is a powerful tool for modern data management.

entry.title: Install/configure Configuration Validation and SOS in Solman
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support, 

I've reported this case, as I cannot find the links/documentation in order to advance with the following:

In order to manage/monitor the security in our managed S/4HANA systems, we want to install and configure the following scenarios in our SAP Solution Manager system "DSM":

1) Configuration Validation
2) Security Optimization Services (SOS)

Our Solution Manager system is installed with release 7.20 Support Package Stack 17:

ST    720    DIGITS    SAPK-72017INSTMAIN    SAP Solution Manager Tool




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Install/configure Configuration Validation and SOS in...
entry.content:
Hi customer,

 

Regarding your query, please find details with below link/note:

1) Configuration Validation

=>https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/techops/3806950748.html#ConfigurationValidationHome-ConfigurationValidationinSAPSolutionManager7.2

2) Security Optimization Services (SOS)

=>

Note :

696478 - SAP Security Optimization: Preparation, additions.

Wiki：

Security Optimization Service - Solution Manager - Support Wiki (sap.com)

Help portal :

SAP Security Optimization Services Portfolio

Sincerely,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Custom Component, Part 2: How we built and shared tooltip component
entry.content:
Hi, @Dan_Wroblewski 

I couldn't have seen this feature for a while, Is this no longer available?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Signavio methodologies - KPIs mapping to Business Pro...
entry.content:
Hey @Gilou ,

I saw the link that was shared by the SAP Team which is connected to Cost and Resouce Analysis from an Execution poin of view, which comes really much more from "Shop Floor Operations" pov.

Although indeed that is also important, what SAP Signavio Process Manager # is not delvering Out-of-the-Box, is attributes for KPIs and PPIs that can be filled in per Process Level, to create a matrix or report and understand what Process Levels are supported by which KPIs. Nor is there a simple way to get a list of best practice KPIs from SAP Best Practices and Import them into SAP Signavio since those attributes are not standard.

So all of this needs to be defined by the organisation with Collaboration of Business Architecture and Process Owners,  and then KPI and PPI attributes need to be created by your admin, and then filled in by your Process owners and Process SMEs for all processes,  in SAP Signavio Process Manager 

Here comes a simple example, creating "Text" fields for KPI and PPI in Custom Attributes (Note: there are other ways that KPIs and PPIs are created as Directory entries and then selected from drop downs, for example)

OK, Lets say you are focusing on the Process E2E (L1): Order to Cash for Automotive.

This E2E L1 has 2 Modular Processes (L2):  1) Order-to-Fulfill   and  2) Invoice to Cash,  each of them decomposes on also on more Process Segments (L3), but lets decompose in L3 for Invoice-to-Cash and then that one into L4s. 

See screen shot 1

In the above example, the organisation has a pretty good understanding of what PPIs and KPIs contribute (bottom-up) to the success of the L2 Invoice to Cash, and thus the related processes need to monitored accoridngly if any of thise PPIs or KPIs are falling below targets.

And they want to ensure that this is also traceble in Signavio, thus all the KPIs need to be associated to the relevant processes. 

First, then new attributes need to be created and assigned to the relevant elements 

See screen shot 2 and 3

Then, when the Signavio admin has assigned the new custom attributes to the relevant elements ( which I recommend should be: BPMN2.0 Diagrams and collapsed processes, as well as Value Chain Diagrams, Processes, Collapsed processes and Groups ), they can be filled in according to all the PPI and KPI work done by the Business Architect and Process Owners. 

See screen shots 4 and 5

 

Then when it is time to design a KPI Dashboard for Order to Cash, all the PPIs and KPIs for all their processes at L4, L3 and L2 need to be included, that way you know how the bottom up metrics are supporting the End to End, and you can trace what PPIs are performing poorly at L4 and identify what is needed to improve processes, people, data or system performance

Not entirely sure if this is what you were looking for, my gut feel told me perhaps, so I hope it inspires or helps a bit.

best!

J.D. Wong-Loera (Business Architect)

entry.title: Re: its possible create SAP notes?
entry.content: Creating SAP notes is doable! SAP notes are like messages or reminders you can write within SAP systems to share info or address issues.

entry.title: Using Migration Cockpit for SAP S/4HANA on add module situation
entry.content:
Hello, 
I'd like to confirm about Migration Cockpit.
I've been joining a project about adding TRM module for the Customer who is already using S/4HANA FI module.
And I want to use the Migration Cockpit to add and to update BP roles in the BP master that is aliready set.
In this case, can I set BP roles, for example "issuer" and so on, for existing BP items?
If its avalable, would you tell me how to use the Migration cockpit?
Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Story Report Calculated Column finding the MAX
entry.content: Hi Jeroen, Thanks for taking time to reply me. Appreciate it very much. This is exactly what I am doing. I am just showing the MAX of number of days as a work around. Another followup question, is there a way to create a calculated dimension date field by adding the number of days to a date? I only see the option of calculated measures in the story report but I don 't find the option of calculated dimension. Any ideas or recommendations to arrive at the MAX date and show in the story? Thanks - Vicky

entry.title: How to recover particular schema from a system backup into another tenant
entry.content:
Hi

We have SAP Business One HANA on a production server and there is a development server (Both are different Physical Servers), we need to restore a particular schema from the system backup of production server to a the Development server with out impacting the existing development server environment.

Kindly help with any notes

entry.title: Re: No entry in ODQMON and DHCDCMON after doing the Initi...
entry.content:
Hi Vinay,

No all scenario will use ODQ interface. Only when DPA (DP agent) is defined in DataSphere, ODP will be used, there'll be entry in ODQMON.

Next question: is DPA used between cloud and Azure? You may check the online help: Preparing Connectivity for Connections | SAP Help Portal

In 1st column 'Connect Type', you can filter using Keywords 'Azure' and choose your scenario. Then check 2nd column, you'll see if DPA is used ('no' or 'yes'). When it's Yes, ODQMON will have entry.  You may also see the attached screen here.

Regarding the entries in DHCDCMON, it's a totally different topic with ODQMON... You would need check from several perspective:

  • DHCDCMON -> Application log -> Is there any error?
  • After CDC init, is there any data change in the source view?
  • Is there any code correction about CDC based on your SAP_BW version?
  • ....

Regards,
Maggie

entry.title: AGING REPORT FOR ACCOUNTS PAYABLE IS NOT WORKING IN SAP S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD TDD.
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

Can we ask your help on how solve attached issue regarding Aging Report for Accounts Payable in our TDD tenant.

Business Group  SAP_FIN_BCG_AP_ANALYTICS_PC and Business Role SAP_BR_AP_MANAGER  is already assigned but still said app is not working.

 

Thanks in Advance!

Regards,
Anj

entry.title: Re: C4C: Contact person set to obsolete when deleted in ERP

entry.title: Re: C4C: Contact person set to obsolete when deleted in ERP
entry.content: Hi Prashanth, We noticed a strange behavior in C4C, where customer in C4C showing as active account though it is set to marked for deletion in ECC.

entry.title: Re: what are the difference between resource from APQC, v...
entry.content:
Hi @ghemraj 

Here comes some insght that might be useful.

APQC, has one generic PCF (Process Classification Framework) which is the cross-industry. 

But they have different categories change for the different PCFs: Retail, Consumer Packaged Goods, Utilites, etc, and thus all the different levels from 2 to 5 also change, for each one.

So you have to analyse which PCF is going to serve you best at your organization, sometimes, I have had to take inspiration from 2 different PCFs to create the final Process Hierarchy. Below is a visual explanation from APQC that might be complimentary to the information that is in the Signavio APQC page:

Shared with permission of APQC

My opinion in relation to Greenfield implementations, is that following the SAP Activate methodolgy and trying to focus to Fit to Standard might be the best way to tackle the process management for SAP Signavio Process Navigator  and SAP Signavio Process Manager  in Explore phase, and then APQC frameworks can indeed help as inspiration to define processes that are gaps resulting from the Fit-to-Standard Analysis workshops, so there process decomposition can serve as guide for to-be process design. 

APQC is an Application Agnostic Framework, since it is created from interviewing hundreds of companies per Industry independent of the applicaiton they run. I believe that APQC is good to ensure that one gets a good inspiration for handovers and level 5 and level 4, but one generally still needs to re design many tasks and some level 4s to suit the companies lingo, organization structure and systems.


Here is a screen shot of the APQC PCF Cross Industry v7.3.1, note that there are Operational Processes and Management and Support Processes.

Best!

JD

entry.title: How to create qr code in smartform
Re: 0FI_ACDOCA_10: Carryforward figures have RCLNT(client...

entry.title: Re: Manage allocation
entry.content: Hi Verena, there is no trainig material for 'Manage Allocation' In Learning Center. Please create one for it.

entry.title: How to check who deleted an inspection plan?
entry.content:
Hi SAP expert,

When we try to open an inspection lot, we have error "Inspection plan DIGITS has been deleted". Nobody know how it is possible. We didn't delete Inspection Plan. And we have feeling, that more Inspection Plan were deleted. Is possibility to find deleted Inspection Plans? And who deleted it and how ? We would like to train users, how to/why NO delete using Inspection Plan. 

Thanks for explanation.

BR and have a nice day


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check who deleted an inspection plan?
entry.content:
Hi,

The CDS view I_InspectionPlanVersion provides access to the header data of inspection plan versions (task list type Q). In this CDS view, you can check whether an inspection plan is deleted by the "Deletion Indicator" field and who did this by "Changed By" field.
You can check the CDS view data in Customer Data Browser app.

BR
SAP Support

entry.title: MRP run on materials shared between plant and MRP area
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have materials (say material A) produced both at the plant (say 0001 Sloc) and the MRP Area (say 0002 Sloc). After the MRP run on requirements for A at both 0001 and 0002, the planned orders on A at both 0001 and 0002 are cascading the dependent requirements on the BOM components (say B and C) only on 0001 Sloc. I can't update the Sloc in BOM as the material header is same (which is A).  We need to have the clear segregation of the requirements of B and C between 0001 and 0002 based on where the planned orders are created for A. Can someone please help on how to achieve this?

Thanks,

Regards, Guru

entry.title: UNLOCK~~$50 Off Temu Coupon{acs789030} or {frw001747} For Existing Customers
entry.content: To get $50 off, sign up as an Existing user using coupon code [ACS789030] and enter the coupon during checkout when making your purchase at Temu. You will receive the benefit once the coupon is applied. The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following: Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests: Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods. Increase the Maximum Number of Connections: You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance. Use a More Efficient HTTP Client: Consider using a more efficient HTTP client library that can handle a larger number of connections without causing the error. Optimize Your Application: Review your application's performance and optimize it to reduce the load on the system. This may involve improving database queries, reducing the number of API calls, or using caching mechanisms. Contact SAP Support: If none of the above solutions work, you can contact SAP support for further assistance. They may be able to provide more specific guidance or help you troubleshoot the issue. Remember to carefully evaluate the impact of any changes you make to your system's configuration to ensure that they do not compromise performance or security.

entry.title: Child Entity Creation without update in RAP
entry.content:
Hi All,

In managed scenario, How can I ensure that a child entity is only creatable and not editable afterward in RESTful ABAP? When I remove the update capability from the behavior definition, the fields become non-editable even during the creation phase. What is the best approach to achieve this effectively? I also tried field (readonly : update) but it does not work.

Kantrice D

entry.title: A Closer Look at the New Metering Metrics added to Metrics Service from SAP HANA Cloud 2024 QRC2
entry.content:
In the relentless quest to boost transparency and allow our users to have a better understanding of their SAP HANA Cloud charges, we are delighted to introduce the new metering metrics to our Metrics Service as part of 2024 QRC2. The primary aim of this update is to give a clear and exact account of the usage of resources.

The addition of metering metrics gives you the ability to pinpoint the charges presently being applied to your system. The metering metrics provides a comprehensive insight into resource usage, which is measured in Capacity Units (CU) for various facets such as memory, storage, compute power, backup and network operations.

An array of system components is under the purview of these metrics, which include default nodes, replica nodes, snapshot nodes, and individual features like Cloud Connector. An exclusive feature of these metrics is that they all return 'delta' type measurements. This implies that the values, which are captured at hourly intervals, measure the changes since the metric value was last taped, giving you an up-to-date report about your consumption.

Metering metrics list

Furthermore, we've ensured the extension of these metrics to both SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database and SAP HANA Cloud, data lake. You can find all the information and details in the official help document here.

How to use metrics service tutorial

To make it convenient for you to consume these metrics via the REST API, we recommend the SAP tutorial "Explore SAP HANA Cloud Alerts and Metrics". You would specifically want to look at the section "Access database metering metrics". Find it here.

Consumption monitor in HANA Cloud Central

Alongside metering metrics availability, we are also thrilled to release the consumption monitor application in HANA Cloud Central. This application lets you monitor consumption in a user-friendly interface, offering you a clear view of your system's consumption. Learn more about using the HANA Cloud Central here.

Conclusion

Armed with these new functionalities, we hope that you'll have a better understanding and grip over your system consumption. We genuinely believe that our constant enhancement will not only lead to more transparency but also help you in making informed strategic decisions. Stay tuned for more updates as we continue to evolve for your needs.
